data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness can strictly beat SC only at k >= 7 with base error e in (1/(k+1), 1/2) and a critic whose per-question precision-recall point lies above a closed-form dominance surface D(e, k, m) = (1-p)^{m} * r * (k-m) - p^{m} * f * m, where m is the SC majority count; the dominance surface is the necessary and sufficient condition.", "mechanism": "For each question, SC returns the majority answer with margin m (count of majority candidates). Harness re-votes after removing critic-flagged candidates. The probability that harness flips a WRONG SC majority to correct is: critic must flag enough majority (wrong) candidates to destroy the majority AND not flag so many minority (right) candidates that the right answer still loses. For a bare majority m = (k+1)/2, this requires flagging at least 2 of the majority (wrong) candidates while flagging 0 of the minority (right) candidates — probability ~ C(2,1) * r^2 * (1-f)^{(k-1)/2}. The probability that harness breaks a CORRECT SC majority is: critic flags enough majority (right) candidates to destroy it — probability ~ C(m, (m+1)/2) * f^{(m+1)/2} * (1-f)^{(m-1)/2}. Harness strictly beats SC when the expected flip-gain exceeds expected break-loss, integrated over the base-error distribution. The dominance surface D specifies this integral as a function of (e, k, p, r, f). Crucially, for k=5 and bare majority m=3, the break probability scales as f^2 while the flip probability scales as r^2 * (1-f), so the condition is r^2 * (1-f) > C * f^2 where C depends on e — explaining why low-precision critics (f ~ 0.875 as in v4-pro hard) destroy harness benefit.", "scope": {"covers": ["odd k >= 5", "base error e in (1/(k+1), 1/2)", "critic precision p and recall r with per-question granularity", "all SC margin values from bare majority to unanimous"], "excludes": ["k < 5 (combinatorial analysis underdetermined)", "base error e <= 1/(k+1) (SC almost never wrong, harness cannot gain)", "base error e >= 1/2 (SC almost always wrong, harness needs near-perfect critic)", "even k (tie-breaking complicates dominance surface)"], "forbidden": [{"case": "Experiment at k=7, e in [0.12, 0.20], critic p=0.60 r=0.80 yields harness accuracy not statistically separable from SC despite D > 0 prediction", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment at k=5, e in [0.06, 0.12], critic p=0.40 r=0.90 yields harness > SC despite D < 0 prediction", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment at k=9, e in [0.08, 0.15], critic p=0.70 r=0.70 yields harness < SC when D > 0 and harness > SC when D < 0 (surface inversion)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "Most mechanistic candidate: derives a closed-form necessary-and-sufficient condition. Unifies 2d711b2e (error-rate bounds) and 7811dbda (fragility window) by showing both are projections of D onto the e-axis. The decisive experiment tests D's predictive power across a grid of (k, e, critic quality) cells, checking whether D > 0 predicts strict harness gain and D < 0 predicts null or loss. Budget: ~280k tokens."}
